title: Mysqldump table with virtual column wrong syntax
description:
Run the following queries:

CREATE TABLE x (c0 BLOB AS ('a') VIRTUAL, c1 INT);
INSERT INTO x(c1) VALUES (1);

Then do mysqldump on the table:

mysqldump -u ... --skip-password sys x

The insert in the output will give:

INSERT INTO `x` (`c1`) VALUES ,1);

The query is not syntactically correct.

The compilation parameters are the same as issue 108148:

-DWITH_DEBUG=1 -DWITH_ASAN=ON -DWITH_UBSAN=ON and boost library version 1.77
How to repeat:
Run the statements above
poc: CREATE TABLE x (c0 BLOB AS ('a') VIRTUAL, c1 INT);
key_bug_features: Mysqldump on table with virtual column generates incorrect syntax
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE y (c0 VARCHAR(255) AS (CONCAT('Value: ', c1)) VIRTUAL, c1 INT NOT NULL, c2 DATE DEFAULT NULL);
CREATE TABLE z (c3 FLOAT, c4 INT, c5 TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT * FROM y WHERE c1 > (SELECT AVG(c4) FROM z);
